violations.text: The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

Open containers of food in prep unit and walk in cooler observed stacked with the top containers in the food below. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (several pair of tongs, and the breading mix container) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Items were taken to be cleaned and sanitized before use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler, and in areas of the kitchen beneath shelves. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

Scoops for some dry ingredients observed stored on an unclean surface (top of the containers) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

Some storage areas observed lined with cardboard and/or aluminum wrap. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

Wood is used in the construction of countertop shelves. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025
Pressed fiberboard at the bottom of a counter unit was not in good repair, shredding and leaving an accumulation of debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the counters and shelf supports have an accumulation of dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/19/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/29/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by stacked buckets. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Open containers of food observed in the walk in cooler, with containers directly on top of the food below. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Tea with milk, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Numerous containers of chicken and eggrolls observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC stated all foods were prepared yesterday. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Tea with milk was placed hot in the refrigerator in covered containers greater than four inches deep. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Raw broccoli was observed stored in used chicken boxes. Food observed stored on the floor in the kitchen and walk in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

Shelves in the walk in cooler are lined with cardboard. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024
The area behind the oil recycling bin has an accumulation of trash and debris. The area must be maintained clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2024

An employee was observed washing their hands at the 3 bay sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by stacked buckets. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Raw animal foods (such as chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked items) in the refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food during storage. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

The chlorine concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 10 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of chlorine in the sanitizing solution must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Rice was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
Food observed stored on the floor in the walk in cooler and kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
Utensils in the kitchen are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
Non-food grade containers are used for food preparation and storage. Food containers must be made of food grade materials. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the tube rack above the cooking area, and the door handle on the walk in cooler have an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
The area behind the grease recycling bin has an accumulation of trash and debris. The area must be maintained clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed along the side of the kitchen. Establishments shall be kept free of items that are not essential to the operation or maintenance of the establishment. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/17/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Chinese Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

An employee was observed washing their hands at the mop sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by stacked buckets. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

Open containers of food in the prep unit and walk in cooler.were observed with the top containers in direct contact with the food below. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Staff failed to sanitize equipment and utensils after washing and rinsing them. After being cleaned, equipment and utensils must be sanitized in the 3-bay-sink or dishwasher. PIC was instructed on 3 bay sink set up. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Garlic in oil observed held at 84 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Chicken and egg rolls observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Rice placed hot in plastic-wrapped containers greater than four inches deep. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024
Spring rolls and rangoons observed stored in cardboard sprout and wine boxes. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Observed employee drying food containers with a cloth towel.Equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Single-use items (old food containers and boxes) are being used for food prep and storage.Single-use items shall not be reused. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Numerous plastic containers were not in good repair, cracked with pieces missing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Walk in shelves observed lined with cardboard. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Drain plugs are lacking for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the table-mounted can opener base, dry ingredient bins, utensil storage bins, and shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024
Hand sink had an accumulation of soil residue. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2024

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the side of the kitchen area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/05/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in areas of the kitchen. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2024

Raw animal foods (such as raw chicken) was observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as fried chicken) in the 2 door refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Refrigerator Separation Storage Poster provided to site. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023
